metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` (or bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`) which `eval_solver.run_case` arm executes a code-review project criterion, what case input key it reads (`diff` or `input`), and whether it reuses the existing code-review arm or requires a new path.
evidence: `_run_criterion_case()` line 110: `plan = case.get('input') or ''` — plan-review finder reads `input` key.
`_run_code_review_case()` line 193: `diff = case.get('diff') or case.get('input') or ''` — code-review reads `diff` key.
Story 225a 'Eval Contract' specifies RP-F1..RP-N6 cases but does not state the case format key (`diff` or `input`) or which solver arm executes them.

ABSENCE: no statement in any ticket or the epic of whether code-review project criteria reuse `_run_criterion_case` (plan-review machinery) or require a new arm routed through the code-review pipeline.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify which `eval_solver.run_case` arm executes a code-review project criterion during `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries`. The plan-review path runs `_run_criterion_case` (using `passes.pass1_chunk` over inline `input` text). The code-review path runs `_run_code_review_case` (using a `diff` field and the full prompt/verifier pipeline). A code-review project criterion is a Pass-1 finder that inspects a diff, but the eval corpus format (input key: `diff` vs `input`), the runner arm, and whether it routes through `_run_criterion_case` (plan-review machinery) or a new `_run_code_review_criterion_case` are entirely unspecified.
findings.location: Bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` Scope / Story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` 'Eval Contract' / `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` dispatch logic
scenarios: Author of `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` uses `diff:` keys; the resolver arm uses `case.get('input')`, silently runs the criterion over empty text, and all 6 must-fire cases return no findings — false `recall=0.0`.

The criterion is incorrectly routed through the plan-review `pass1_chunk` path, which lacks code-review context (no diff, no changed-file metadata), causing the LLM to hallucinate or abstain on all cases.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add brief scenario descriptors for each of the twelve eval cases (RP-F1..RP-F6 / RP-N1..RP-N6) directly in the plan — one sentence per case is enough to uniquely identify the phase-boundary scenario each case exercises and let any executor author consistent inputs.
evidence: 'add … a balanced RP-F1..RP-F6 / RP-N1..RP-N6 eval corpus' — the IDs are named but no scenario descriptors are given
ABSENCE: no table, appendix, or inline description maps any RP-* ID to a concrete input fragment or boundary scenario
'prove the distinction with the balanced eval corpus before live rollout' — the corpus is load-bearing for the rollout gate but its content is executor-invented
findings.finding: The twelve eval corpus cases (RP-F1..RP-F6 and RP-N1..RP-N6) are referenced by ID throughout the plan but their content — the specific scenario each case encodes — is never described. An executing agent must invent all twelve cases from scratch, guided only by the Four-Phase Contract prose. Different executors will produce qualitatively different corpora, undermining the stated goal of a 'balanced' corpus that 'proves the distinction' and meets the 1.0/0.0/0.70 thresholds reliably.
findings.location: Scope item 2 ('Author the project criterion — 225a-323c-a9f6-436b') and AC bullet 4 ('emits findings for RP-F1..RP-F6, and emits none for RP-N1..RP-N6')
scenarios: Executor A covers only Pass-1→Pass-4 boundary violations; executor B covers only Pass-3 LLM-participation violations; the corpora are incommensurable and neither may exercise all six negative-test axes.
A future maintainer modifying the criterion cannot tell whether a corpus change preserves the original intent.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small table or list in the 'Scope' section under story 225a, e.g.: RP-F1 — Pass-1 finder emits a severity judgment; RP-F2 — Pass-1 finder prescribes a specific implementation fix; RP-F3 — Pass-2 verifier introduces a new candidate finding; RP-F4 — Pass-3 decision step invokes an LLM; RP-F5 — Pass-4 coach dictates an exact implementation; RP-F6 — Pass-2 verifier changes a finding's disposition. RP-N1..RP-N6 mirror these with compliant phrasing (e.g., RP-N1 — Pass-1 finder tightens evidence requirements without assessing validity). Exact wording is up to the author.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in both the Scope (seam story) and AC 6 whether `criteria_routing.json` stores entries under logical IDs (`project.review-phase-boundaries`) or physical IDs (`code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`), and ensure the AC 6 Python snippet uses the form that matches the actual JSON schema.
evidence: Scope: 'The repair maps logical `project.<name>` ids to validator-safe physical `code-review-project-<name>` ids'
AC 6: `r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]` — uses logical ID as JSON key
No section of the plan specifies which ID form is used as the key in `criteria_routing.json`
findings.finding: AC 6 uses the logical ID `project.review-phase-boundaries` as the key in `criteria_routing.json`, but the Scope section states the seam repair maps logical `project.<name>` IDs to physical `code-review-project-<name>` IDs. The plan does not specify whether `criteria_routing.json` is keyed by logical or physical IDs, so AC 6's verify step may reference the wrong key if the JSON uses physical IDs.
findings.location: Scope section (seam story description) vs. AC 6 (Python verify snippet)
scenarios: An implementer writes `criteria_routing.json` with physical keys (consistent with the mapping description) and AC 6's verify step raises a KeyError, failing a correct implementation.
An implementer writes `criteria_routing.json` with logical keys (consistent with AC 6) but the internal seam routing breaks because it expects physical keys.
findings.suggested_fix: If `criteria_routing.json` is keyed by logical IDs (recommended for human readability and consistency with AC 6), add a sentence to the seam story: 'The routing JSON continues to use logical IDs as keys; the logical→physical translation is performed internally by the seam at dispatch time.' If keyed by physical IDs, update AC 6's snippet to `["code_review"]["code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries"]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a verification step for 'runtime documentation' to AC 3 (e.g., `rebar criteria --help` or a doc-generation smoke test), or remove 'runtime documentation' from the seam story's deliverable list if it is not separately verifiable.
evidence: Scope item 1: 'deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval/CLI resolution, compatibility coverage, and runtime documentation'

AC 3: '...runs it once in Pass 1, applies repository-effective Pass-3 routing, resolves its eval spec through the CLI, rejects unknown ids, and preserves overlay-absent behavior' — no documentation check
findings.finding: The seam story scope lists 'runtime documentation' as an explicit deliverable, but AC 3 has no corresponding verification step for documentation — it covers fan-in, routing, CLI resolution, unknown-id rejection, and overlay-absent behavior only.
scenarios: Runtime documentation (e.g., --help text, auto-generated schema docs) is omitted by the implementer because no AC enforces its presence; the deliverable silently regresses.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit audit/migration step (or an expand-contract note confirming no existing config uses the current physical id format) for `criteria_routing.json` entries affected by the `project.<name>` → `code-review-project-<name>` remapping, so existing routing consumers are not silently broken.
evidence: 'project routing can merge, but a net-new LLM-backed project criterion is not dynamically fanned into Pass 1…' — confirms routing is partially operational, implying existing configs may use the current id format

The rollback plan ('revert the generic fan-in/routing/eval repair as one unit') handles full rollback but does not address partial migration of pre-existing routing entries that use the current physical id convention
No audit step or expand-contract sequence is described for existing `criteria_routing.json` consumers of the old id format
findings.finding: The id-remapping change (logical `project.<name>` → physical `code-review-project-<name>`) is an interface change to `criteria_routing.json` entries. The plan states 'project routing can merge' today, meaning some routing configs may already reference the current id format. The plan does not describe expand-contract sequencing or a migration/audit step for existing routing entries that could silently break when the remapping is applied.
findings.location: Scope section, item 1: '…maps logical project.<name> ids to validator-safe physical code-review-project-<name> ids…'
scenarios: A repository whose `criteria_routing.json` already has an entry keyed to the old physical id format (because routing merging worked) silently loses routing after the remapping lands, with no migration guidance.
A CI pipeline that passes today because routing merges but other integration points are broken starts failing differently after the id remapping, with no expand-contract path to fall back.
findings.suggested_fix: Before landing the remapping, scan all `criteria_routing.json` files in the repository for keys matching the current physical id pattern and either migrate them automatically or document in the Scope section that no such entries exist (citing the scan result). Add this as a precondition in the seam bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in Scope item 1 whether ADRs 0015, 0016, and/or 0017 require amendment to reflect the new fan-in/routing/eval-resolution behavior, and specify the form and location of the 'runtime documentation' deliverable (e.g., updated ADR, new ADR section, or named doc file).
evidence: 'deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval/CLI resolution, compatibility coverage, and runtime documentation' — names 'runtime documentation' without specifying what it is or where it lives

'dogfoods the existing project-criteria extension described by ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017' — implies those ADRs are the authoritative reference, but the seam changes extend/fix their described behavior, potentially making them stale
ABSENCE: no statement that ADRs 0015/0016/0017 will be reviewed for staleness or updated
findings.finding: The generic seam changes (dynamic fan-in into Pass 1, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, CLI/eval resolution) constitute an architectural extension of the system described by ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017. The plan does not specify whether those ADRs require amendment or whether a new ADR captures the updated contract. The 'runtime documentation' deliverable is named in Scope item 1 but is unspecified in form, location, and scope — leaving it unclear whether it covers the changed architectural contract.
findings.location: Scope section, item 1 ('…runtime documentation…'); Context section ('dogfoods the existing project-criteria extension described by ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017')
scenarios: A future contributor reads ADR 0015/0016/0017 to understand the extension contract, finds it does not describe dynamic fan-in or the new id remapping, and implements a new criterion against the stale contract.
The 'runtime documentation' deliverable is interpreted narrowly (e.g., a CLI --help update) and the architectural change goes undocumented at the ADR level.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or amend ADRs 0015/0016/0017) to document the architectural decisions introduced by the seam repair: dynamic fan-in strategy, physical-id mapping convention (`project.<name>` → `code-review-project-<name>`), Pass-3 repository-effective routing design, and CLI eval resolution surface.
evidence: 'One generic core bug is included because inspection of origin/main found that the advertised code-review extension seam is incomplete: project routing can merge, but a net-new LLM-backed project criterion is not dynamically fanned into Pass 1, resolved by the eval surface, or consulted through repository-aware routing in Pass 3.'
'deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval/CLI resolution, compatibility coverage, and runtime documentation'

Plan says 'dogfoods the existing project-criteria extension described by ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017' but the seam being repaired is explicitly incomplete — meaning those ADRs do not fully cover the new architecture being delivered.
ABSENCE: no mention of authoring a new ADR or updating an existing one for the seam repair decisions.
findings.finding: The generic seam repair (story 2c14 + bug 8a4b) introduces novel architectural changes to rebar core — dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, and CLI eval resolution — that go beyond what ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017 originally specified (since those ADRs describe an extension seam that this plan explicitly identifies as incomplete). No new or amended ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is planned to capture this architectural decision.
scenarios: A future contributor reading ADRs 0015-0017 will see the advertised extension contract but not the actual fan-in, routing, and eval-resolution decisions introduced in this epic, making it harder to safely extend or debug the seam.

If the fan-in or Pass-3 routing logic needs to change, the rationale (why the physical id mapping was chosen, why the eval CLI surface was extended this way) is undocumented, increasing regression risk.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new ADR (e.g., 0018) or an addendum section in ADR 0015 that records: (1) the identified incompleteness and its root cause, (2) the chosen fan-in mechanism and why, (3) the id-translation convention and its rationale, (4) Pass-3 routing approach, and (5) overlay-absent preservation guarantee. Reference this ADR from the story 2c14 acceptance criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace bare `.venv/bin/<cmd>` references in every AC verification command with a platform-neutral invocation (e.g. `python -m pytest`, `python -m rebar`, or a Makefile/script target) so the ACs are verifiable on Windows as well as POSIX.
evidence: ".venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py"
".venv/bin/rebar list --parent jira-reb-1001 --output llm"
".venv/bin/python -c 'import json; r=json.load(...)'"
On Windows, `python -m venv .venv` creates `.venv\Scripts\` not `.venv/bin/`; `.venv/bin/rebar` is a missing path.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria verification commands hard-code `.venv/bin/` paths (POSIX virtual-environment layout), which do not exist on Windows where the equivalent directory is `.venv\Scripts\`. A developer or CI runner on Windows who attempts to verify any AC will get a path-not-found error, and the ACs will be unverifiable in that environment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five verifiable ACs (`.venv/bin/pytest`, `.venv/bin/rebar`, `.venv/bin/python`)
scenarios: Windows developer workstation: running any AC verification command fails immediately with `The system cannot find the path specified` or equivalent, making the entire acceptance gate unexecutable on a supported platform.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `.venv/bin/pytest` → `python -m pytest`, `.venv/bin/rebar` → `python -m rebar` (or the installed console-script equivalent), and `.venv/bin/python` → `python` throughout the AC section. Alternatively, add a note that a POSIX shell or WSL is required for verification and document a Windows-equivalent command alongside each POSIX form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the gate-aware prompt-id mapping interface in bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` — e.g., whether `criterion_prompt_id()` in `ids.py` gains a `gate` parameter, gains a `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` sibling, or is replaced by a gate-dispatching function — so the seam implementer and the criterion story implementer cannot produce incompatible physical ids independently.
evidence: `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str` in `ids.py` has no gate parameter; it always returns `f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — the prefix is `plan-review-` unconditionally.

Bug 8a4b scope: 'Add a gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping for code-review project criteria: `project.<name>` maps to `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-replaced-by-dashes>`.'

The plan never states whether this means adding a `gate` parameter to `criterion_prompt_id()`, creating a separate `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()`, or replacing the function with a gate-dispatching variant.

Story 225a independently authors the prompt at `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` (physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`), which is only correct if the mapping resolves to `code-review-` prefix — but without a specified function signature, an implementer of 8a4b could produce `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` and never discover the mismatch until the CLI eval fails.
findings.finding: The plan names `ids.py` as the seam for 'gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping' and specifies that `project.<name>` must map to `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-replaced-by-dashes>` for code-review criteria, but the existing `criterion_prompt_id()` function in `ids.py` takes only a `criterion_id` with no gate parameter and unconditionally produces a `plan-review-` prefix. The plan does not specify HOW to extend this function — whether to add a gate parameter, create a parallel `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` function, use a dispatch table, or replace the function entirely. Without this specification, an implementer of bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` must invent the interface design, and an implementer of story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` (who independently authors the prompt at the `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` physical id) may choose an incompatible path, leaving the two implementations unable to resolve each other's id.
findings.location: Bug 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 — Scope ('gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping') and Implementation Notes ('Likely seams are `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`'); `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py::criterion_prompt_id()`
scenarios: Bug 8a4b implementer adds a gate parameter to `criterion_prompt_id()` but story 225a implementer calls the old no-gate form, silently getting a `plan-review-` prefixed id that resolves to a missing prompt file.

Bug 8a4b implementer creates a new `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` function but the eval_solver and batch_runner fixes call the old `criterion_prompt_id()`, producing wrong prompt ids for Pass-1 code-review project criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the bug's Implementation Notes specifying the interface change: e.g., 'Add a `gate: str` parameter to `criterion_prompt_id()` (or a `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` sibling) so callers pass `gate="code_review"` to produce the `code-review-` prefix; update every caller in `batch_runner.py`, `eval_solver.py`, and `workflow_ops.py` to pass the gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `calibrate_criterion()` in `eval.py` (and its `criterion_prompt_id` call) is included in bug 8a4b's scope and file_impact, so the eval path resolves `project.review-phase-boundaries` to the `code-review-project-*` prefix rather than `plan-review-project-*`.
evidence: ids.py `criterion_prompt_id()` always returns `f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — no gate parameter.

eval.py `calibrate_criterion()` line 416-420: `prompt_id = criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — calls `criterion_prompt_id` with no gate context.
Plan Scope item 1: 'The repair maps logical `project.<name>` ids to validator-safe physical `code-review-project-<name>` ids'.

Bug 8a4b scope: 'Add a gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping for code-review project criteria: `project.<name>` maps to `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-replaced-by-dashes>`'.
Story 2c14 file_impact lists `ids.py` but does NOT list `eval.py` (`calibrate_criterion`) as a file requiring change, creating a risk the eval path is missed.
Evidence class: Verified (observed in source code).
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id()` in `ids.py` unconditionally produces a `plan-review-` prefix, and `calibrate_criterion()` in `eval.py` calls it without a gate parameter. The plan asserts `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` will resolve the eval spec at `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, but the current call chain (`_criteria_eval` → `calibrate_criterion` → `criterion_prompt_id`) will instead look for `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, silently failing to find the correct eval file.
findings.location: Scope item 1 / Acceptance Criteria (eval AC) / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:416-420 / src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-41
scenarios: Developer runs `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` after landing story 2c14 — `calibrate_criterion` computes prompt_id as `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`, looks for the wrong eval file, raises EvalError, and the AC verification command fails.
The epic AC and story 225a-323c-a9f6-436b AC both cite this exact command as their verification step — a silent mismatch here would mask the defect until live execution.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py` to bug 8a4b's `file_impact` list and explicitly state that `calibrate_criterion` must accept a `gate` parameter (or infer it from whether the criterion id appears in the code-review effective registry) before calling `criterion_prompt_id`, so `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml` is the resolved path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the gate-aware prompt-id mapping protocol in bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`: name the function (or extension to `criterion_prompt_id`), its module location, what gate context it receives, and the exact rule that produces `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-as-dashes>` for code-review vs `plan-review-project-<name>` for plan-review.
evidence: `ids.py` line 42: `return f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — prefix is `plan-review-` unconditionally, no gate parameter.
No `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant exists anywhere in the codebase (confirmed by search).
Plan Scope item 1: 'The repair maps logical `project.<name>` ids to validator-safe physical `code-review-project-<name>` ids' — states the desired output but not the protocol.

Bug 8a4b Scope: 'Add a gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping for code-review project criteria' — names the deliverable but gives no function signature, module location, or gate-threading rule.
Story 225a file_impact lists prompt at `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` implying prefix `code-review-`, which `criterion_prompt_id()` cannot produce.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a 'gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping' for code-review project criteria, but no processing protocol is defined: which function/module owns it, what gate parameter it receives, and what rule produces `code-review-project-<name>` rather than `plan-review-project-<name>`. The existing `criterion_prompt_id()` in `ids.py` is gate-unaware — it unconditionally prepends `plan-review-` — and no `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant or gate-keyed dispatch exists anywhere in the codebase. An implementer of bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` must invent the mapping rule and its location without a spec.
findings.location: Epic Scope item 1 / Bug 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 Scope ('Add a gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping') / `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`
scenarios: Implementer A adds a gate parameter to `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate=None)` in `ids.py`; implementer B adds a separate `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` in `batch_runner.py`; the two approaches diverge and the eval resolver uses the wrong one.

Implementer uses the existing `criterion_prompt_id()` unchanged (producing `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`), prompt resolves from wrong path, criterion silently never fires through Pass 1.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `gate` parameter to `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate='plan_review')` returning `f'code-review-project-{criterion_id[len("project."):].replace(".", "-")}' if gate == 'code_review' else f'plan-review-{criterion_id.replace(".", "-")}'`, and specify in the bug that all call sites in the code-review batch runner and eval resolver use `gate='code_review'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` a concrete protocol for the `_code_review_prompt_id` extension: specify the inverse mapping rule (physical `code-review-project-<name>` → logical `project.<name>`) and the exact API call used to confirm the logical id is active (e.g., `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` membership check).
evidence: `_code_review_prompt_id()` line 173–176: `valid = {"code-review-base", "code-review-verify"} | {f"code-review-{oid}" for oid in OVERLAY_IDS}; return prompt_id if prompt_id in valid else None` — completely closed, no project-criterion path.

Bug 8a4b Scope: 'Extend the existing criterion-eval resolver to recognize activated code-review project criteria and load their `.rebar/evals/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml` specs' — no inverse-mapping rule given.
Plan-review analogue `_criterion_id()` lines 66–88: strips `plan-review-` prefix, calls `registry.by_id(repo_root)` — a plan-review-specific function unavailable on the code-review side.

ABSENCE: no specification of which function confirms a code-review project id is active, or how the physical `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` id is inverted to `project.review-phase-boundaries` for the registry check.
findings.finding: The eval resolver's `_code_review_prompt_id()` is a closed set (`{code-review-base, code-review-verify} ∪ {code-review-<oid>}`) and the plan says it must be extended to accept activated code-review project criteria. However, no protocol is specified for (a) how to map a physical code-review project prompt id back to its logical `project.<name>` id, or (b) how to confirm that logical id is active in `code_review.effective_criteria(repo_root)`. The existing plan-review analogue (`_criterion_id`) uses `plan-review-` prefix stripping then registry lookup — but the code-review path has no `registry.by_id(repo_root)` returning project-criterion descriptors, only `effective_criteria()` returning a tuple of active ids.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` lines 162–176 (`_code_review_prompt_id`); Bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` Scope ('Extend the existing criterion-eval resolver'); Story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` AC bullet 5
scenarios: Implementer adds a prefix-strip that recovers `review-phase-boundaries` from `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` and calls a nonexistent `code_review.registry.by_id()`, raising AttributeError at runtime.
Implementer uses a prefix-match alone (as the bug text warns against: 'a prefix match alone is insufficient') and accidentally admits inactive or misnamed ids.

findings.finding: The `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries` command will fail at the CLI pre-flight guard in `_llm_eval_commands.py` (line 147) even after bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` is fixed. That guard checks `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)`, which only knows plan-review criteria — a code-review-only project criterion will never appear there and the command returns exit-1 with 'unknown criterion' before reaching `eval_solver`. Neither the bug ticket's `file_impact` list nor the parent story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` names `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py` as a repair seam, leaving this blocking CLI-layer gap unaddressed.
findings.location: Scope item 1 (seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`) / bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` file_impact list / epic-level AC: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`
findings.suggested_fix: In `_criteria_eval`, extend the pre-flight guard to check the code-review effective criteria when the plan-review registry lookup misses: `from rebar.llm.code_review.registry import effective_criteria as cr_criteria; if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root) and args.criterion_id not in cr_criteria(repo_root): ...`

findings.finding: The plan names the project criterion's prompt file as `code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md` (containing a dot), but the runtime prompt-id validator (`_valid_id` = `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`) rejects any id containing a dot, causing `get_prompt()` to raise an error when the criterion's prompt is loaded.
findings.location: Epic file_impact: `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`; criterion story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` file_impact same entry; `ids.py` module docstring establishes the plan-review analog is `plan-review-project-<name>` (dot→dash).
findings.suggested_fix: In `ids.py` (or a new code-review analog), define the code-review project criterion prompt-id mapping as `project.<name>` → `code-review-project-<name>` (single dot replaced by a dash, same rule as plan-review). Update the file_impact entries in the epic and criterion story to `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md`, the eval spec to `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, and the AC routing check to reference the dash form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit precondition to story 9447 confirming the Gerrit review bot is deployed and configured to run against the rebar repository, including any auth/credential requirements, or name the spike/prerequisite task that establishes this before story 9447 begins.
evidence: Plan: 'Run an advisory-only Gerrit dogfood probe after the seam repair and project criterion land. Use two real patchsets against the review-system prompt surface.'
Story 9447: 'the deployed Gerrit review bot loads the criterion from the reviewed rebar repository' — no deployment URL, credential model, or confirmation of bot availability stated.
AC: '[operator-attested] A positive-control Gerrit patchset is recorded with change id, patchset SHA, UTC timestamp…' — the entire AC relies on a live Gerrit bot run.
No auth/HTTPS preconditions for the Gerrit integration are stated anywhere in the plan or child tickets.
Evidence class: Unverified (the bot's existence and configuration relative to the rebar repo cannot be confirmed from plan text or codebase inspection alone).
findings.finding: The Gerrit review bot integration in story 9447 is an unverified external capability: the plan asserts the 'deployed Gerrit review bot loads the criterion from the reviewed rebar repository and carries it through the real four-pass pipeline', but states no auth preconditions, no confirmation the bot is already deployed and configured to run against rebar's own repository, and no fallback if the bot is unavailable or misconfigured at dogfood time.
findings.location: Scope item 3 / story 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d / Risks and Rollout section
scenarios: Story 9447 is ready to execute but the Gerrit bot has not been configured to review the rebar repository (or requires re-deployment after the seam change) — the operator-attested ACs block on an infrastructure dependency not gated in the plan.
The bot exists but requires separate credential provisioning (API key, OAuth scope) that is not documented as a precondition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the epic title (or add a subtitle/scope summary) to reflect that the work includes a generic core integration-seam repair (stories 2c14 + bug 8a4b) in addition to the project criterion, so the full delivery scope is discoverable without reading the full body.
evidence: Title: 'Add a project level code review criteria that enforces the separation of concerns in our 4 phase review process'
Plan Context: 'One generic core bug is included because inspection of origin/main found that the advertised code-review extension seam is incomplete'

Scope item 1: 'Complete the generic integration seam — 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02: deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval/CLI resolution, compatibility coverage, and runtime documentation'
The seam fix benefits ALL project criteria, not only project.review-phase-boundaries
findings.finding: The plan title ('Add a project level code review criteria that enforces the separation of concerns in our 4 phase review process') scopes to a single project criterion, but the plan body also delivers a generic core seam repair (stories 2c14-fa5f and bug 8a4b-e8b6) that affects all project criteria system-wide, not just this one. A reader relying on the title alone will be surprised by the broader infrastructure scope, and the title does not signal that a core-platform change is included.
findings.location: Title vs. Scope section and stories 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02, 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8
scenarios: A product manager or stakeholder reading only the title approves work expecting a single criterion addition, then discovers two generic infrastructure stories and a core bug fix in the delivery — creating scope-control or communication friction.

If the seam stories are later reorganized or deprioritized independently (the plan notes 'both precede live Gerrit dogfooding'), the title gives no signal that the criterion story 225a is blocked on core platform work.
findings.suggested_fix: Retitle to something like: 'Add project.review-phase-boundaries criterion and repair generic project-criteria integration seam (prerequisite)' — or add a one-sentence scope note directly under the title calling out the seam repair as an included prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to 225a requiring its change landed on main (git log grep for 225a-323c-a9f6-436b), and add an AC to 9447 requiring its change landed on main (git log grep for 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d), matching the verification already present in 2c14 AC-1 for 8a4b.
evidence: Parent AC-2: 'The bug, criterion, and dogfood changes are landed on main; verify commits with git log origin/main --oneline --grep=\'rebar-ticket: (8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8|225a-323c-a9f6-436b|9447-b31c-e0b7-446d)\''
Child 2c14 AC-1: 'Child bug 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 is closed and its change is landed on main; verify with git log origin/main --oneline --grep=\'rebar-ticket: 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8\''
Child 225a ACs: no acceptance criterion mentions landing on main or git log verification for the 225a commit itself
Child 9447 ACs: no acceptance criterion mentions landing on main or git log verification for the 9447 commit itself
Sibling roster cross-check: all three siblings reviewed; none covers git-log-on-main for 225a or 9447
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Parent AC-2 requires git-log-on-main verification for all three ticket commits (8a4b, 225a, 9447) via a single grep command; child 2c14 explicitly includes 'its change is landed on main' as an AC-1 done-criterion for 8a4b, but neither 225a nor 9447 contain an acceptance criterion requiring their own change to land on main. This is a partial-without-remainder: the 225a and 9447 landing-on-main remainder is owned by no child story.
scenarios: 225a closes after its unit tests and eval attestation pass but its PR is not merged; parent AC-2 fails at epic close because the git log grep for 225a-323c-a9f6-436b returns nothing
9447 is operator-attested without a merged commit; the parent's git log command fails the rebar-ticket grep for 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
The completion verifier runs the parent's AC-2 git log command and finds only the 8a4b entry, blocking epic closure even though all child ACs are individually satisfied
findings.suggested_fix: In child 225a, add: '[ ] The criterion change is landed on main; verify with `git log origin/main --oneline --grep="rebar-ticket: 225a-323c-a9f6-436b"`'. In child 9447, add: '[ ] The dogfood change is landed on main; verify with `git log origin/main --oneline --grep="rebar-ticket: 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d"`'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
